methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $14.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C AI & Software company founded by Brett Adcock, has raised over $1B and carries a current post-money valuation of $39.0B. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private firms and one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) spanning frontier AI and robotics, including same-stage and adjacent-stage peers such as Skild AI ($14.0B Series C), Wayve ($8.6B Series D), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B Series B), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B Series B).

Two valuation methods were applied to triangulate fair value. Stage-progression analysis, which applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next funding stage, implies a valuation of $66.3B based on the $39.0B last post-money. Peer-median methodology across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $7.1B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $14.0B. The divergence between these methods reflects the substantial spread observed in AI & Software valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $76.2B, with a median implied value of $36.7B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around both stage-based multiples in a rapidly evolving frontier AI market and appropriate peer selection given the heterogeneity of business models and technical maturity across the comparable set. Current valuation sits at the upper end of the defensible range.
